---
title: "qPCR Host-DNA Depletion Analysis - IMPROVED"
author: "Your Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: flatly
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 8
)
```

```{r load libraries}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(rstatix)
library(readxl)
library(patchwork)  # For combining plots
```

# Import and organize data

```{r load data}
# Read the data
data <- read_excel('qPCR_host_depletion_chicken_all.xlsx')

# View structure
glimpse(data)
```

```{r clean data}
# Clean and structure the data
data_clean <- data %>%
  # Remove rows with all NA values
  filter(!is.na(Name)) %>%
  # Re-label "No Host-DNA Depletion No. 3" and "Host-DNA Depletion No. 3"
  # as cheesecloth replicate 4
  mutate(
    Name = case_when(
      Name == "No Host-DNA Depletion No. 3" ~ "Cheesecloth No Host 4",
      Name == "Host-DNA Depletion No. 3"    ~ "Cheesecloth Host-DNA Depletion 4",
      TRUE                                  ~ Name
    )
  ) %>%
  # Extract sample information
  mutate(
    Treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(Name, "No Host") ~ "Before Depletion",
      str_detect(Name, "Host-DNA Depletion|\\bHost\\b") ~ "After Depletion",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    Treatment = factor(Treatment, levels = c("Before Depletion", "After Depletion")),
    Sample_Type = case_when(
      str_detect(Name, "cheesecloth|Cheesecloth") ~ "Cheesecloth",
      str_detect(Name, "RVS") ~ "RVS",
      str_detect(Name, "CAT") ~ "CAT",
      str_detect(Name, "Cent \\+ Filt") ~ "Centrifuge + Filter",
      str_detect(Name, "Filt") & !str_detect(Name, "Cent") ~ "Filter Only",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    Replicate_ID = str_extract(Name, "\\d+$"),
    Replicate_ID = as.numeric(Replicate_ID)
  ) %>%
  select(Name, Treatment, Sample_Type, Replicate_ID, Ct, `Calc Conc (ng/ul)`) %>%
  rename(Concentration = `Calc Conc (ng/ul)`)

# View cleaned data summary
data_clean %>% 
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment) %>% 
  summarise(
    Total_Measurements = n(),
    Detected = sum(!is.na(Concentration)),
    Non_Detects = sum(is.na(Concentration)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(caption = "Data Overview by Sample Type and Treatment")
```

```{r prepare summary stats}
# Set detection limits
detection_limit_ct <- 40       # Standard qPCR detection limit
detection_limit_conc <- 0.0001 # Very low concentration (0.1 pg/μL)

# Calculate summary statistics by biological replicate
# For non-detects, we'll handle them separately in statistical tests
summary_stats <- data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Below_Detection = is.na(Ct),
    # Flag samples where ALL technical replicates are non-detects
    Sample_ID = paste(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID, sep = "_")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_measurements = n(),
    n_detected = sum(!Below_Detection),
    n_non_detect = sum(Below_Detection),
    All_Non_Detect = all(Below_Detection),
    Mean_Ct = ifelse(all(is.na(Ct)), NA_real_, mean(Ct, na.rm = TRUE)),
    SD_Ct = ifelse(all(is.na(Ct)), NA_real_, sd(Ct, na.rm = TRUE)),
    SE_Ct = ifelse(all(is.na(Ct)), NA_real_, SD_Ct / sqrt(sum(!is.na(Ct)))),
    Mean_Conc = ifelse(all(is.na(Concentration)), NA_real_, mean(Concentration, na.rm = TRUE)),
    SD_Conc = ifelse(all(is.na(Concentration)), NA_real_, sd(Concentration, na.rm = TRUE)),
    SE_Conc = ifelse(all(is.na(Concentration)), NA_real_, SD_Conc / sqrt(sum(!is.na(Concentration)))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Display summary table
summary_stats %>%
  select(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID, n_detected, n_non_detect, 
         All_Non_Detect, Mean_Ct, Mean_Conc) %>%
  arrange(Sample_Type, Replicate_ID, Treatment) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 2, caption = "Summary Statistics by Biological Replicate")
```

# Cheesecloth Samples - COMBINED PANEL PLOT

```{r cheesecloth combined panel plot}
# Prepare cheesecloth data for plotting
cheese_data <- data_clean %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth") %>%
  mutate(
    Below_Detection = is.na(Ct),
    # For plotting, assign detection limit values to non-detects
    Ct_plot = ifelse(is.na(Ct), detection_limit_ct, Ct),
    Conc_plot = ifelse(is.na(Concentration), detection_limit_conc, Concentration)
  )

# Define color palette (matching your previous plots)
color_palette <- c("1" = "#fc8d62", "2" = "#4DBBD5", 
                   "3" = "#66c2a5", "4" = "#e78ac3")

# Create long format data for faceting
cheese_long <- cheese_data %>%
  select(Treatment, Replicate_ID, Below_Detection, Ct_plot, Conc_plot) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Ct_plot, Conc_plot),
    names_to = "Measurement",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Measurement = factor(Measurement,
                        levels = c("Ct_plot", "Conc_plot"),
                        labels = c("Ct Value", 
                                  "DNA Concentration (ng/µL) - log10 scale"))
  )

# Create the combined panel plot
cheese_panel_plot <- ggplot(cheese_long,
                            aes(x = Treatment, y = Value,
                                color = as.factor(Replicate_ID),
                                group = as.factor(Replicate_ID))) +
  # Mean values as lines connecting before/after
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  # Mean values as points
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  # Error bars (SE)
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", 
               width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  # Individual technical replicates (with shape for non-detects)
  geom_jitter(aes(shape = Below_Detection), alpha = 0.5, 
              width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2.5) +
  # Facet by measurement type
  facet_wrap(~ Measurement, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y", strip.position = "left") +
  # Use log scale for concentration panel only
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_palette,
                     name = "Biological\nReplicate") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 16, "TRUE" = 4),
                     labels = c("Detected", "Non-detect"),
                     name = "Detection\nStatus") +
  labs(
    title = "Cheesecloth Filtered Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy",
    subtitle = "Each point = qPCR technical replicate | Lines connect mean ± SE | Concentration on log scale",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

print(cheese_panel_plot)

ggsave("cheesecloth_combined_panels.png", cheese_panel_plot,
       width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

# Statistical Analysis with Non-Detection Handling

```{r stats with non-detections}
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("STATISTICAL ANALYSIS - CHEESECLOTH\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Prepare paired data
cheese_paired <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth") %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, Treatment, Mean_Ct, Mean_Conc, All_Non_Detect) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Treatment, 
              values_from = c(Mean_Ct, Mean_Conc, All_Non_Detect))

cat("Paired Data Structure:\n")
print(cheese_paired)
cat("\n")

# Extract before/after vectors
before_conc <- cheese_paired$`Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`
after_conc <- cheese_paired$`Mean_Conc_After Depletion`
before_ct <- cheese_paired$`Mean_Ct_Before Depletion`
after_ct <- cheese_paired$`Mean_Ct_After Depletion`

# Identify which samples have non-detects
after_nondetect <- cheese_paired$`All_Non_Detect_After Depletion`

cat("Non-detection status:\n")
for (i in 1:nrow(cheese_paired)) {
  cat(sprintf("Replicate %d: Before = %s, After = %s\n",
              cheese_paired$Replicate_ID[i],
              ifelse(is.na(before_conc[i]), "ND", "detected"),
              ifelse(is.na(after_conc[i]), "ND", "detected")))
}
cat("\n")

# ============================================================================
# APPROACH 1: EXCLUDE NON-DETECTS (Conservative)
# ============================================================================
cat("========================================\n")
cat("APPROACH 1: Excluding Non-Detects\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Filter to only complete pairs
complete_pairs <- !is.na(before_conc) & !is.na(after_conc)
n_complete <- sum(complete_pairs)

cat("Complete pairs (both detected):", n_complete, "\n\n")

if (n_complete >= 2) {
  before_complete <- before_conc[complete_pairs]
  after_complete <- after_conc[complete_pairs]
  
  # Paired t-test
  t_result <- t.test(before_complete, after_complete,
                     paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")
  
  cat("PAIRED T-TEST (excluding non-detects):\n")
  cat("  n pairs:", n_complete, "\n")
  cat("  Mean Before:", round(mean(before_complete), 3), "ng/μL\n")
  cat("  Mean After:", round(mean(after_complete), 3), "ng/μL\n")
  cat("  t-statistic:", round(t_result$statistic, 3), "\n")
  cat("  df:", t_result$parameter, "\n")
  cat("  p-value:", format.pval(t_result$p.value, digits = 4), "\n")
  
  if (t_result$p.value < 0.05) {
    cat("  *** SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05) ***\n")
  } else if (t_result$p.value < 0.10) {
    cat("  † Trend toward significance (p < 0.10)\n")
  } else {
    cat("  Not significant (p ≥ 0.10)\n")
  }
  
  # Effect size
  differences <- before_complete - after_complete
  cohens_d <- mean(differences) / sd(differences)
  fold_change <- mean(before_complete) / mean(after_complete)
  
  cat("\nEFFECT SIZE:\n")
  cat("  Fold reduction:", round(fold_change, 2), "×\n")
  cat("  Cohen's d:", round(cohens_d, 2), "\n\n")
  
  # Wilcoxon test
  w_result <- wilcox.test(before_complete, after_complete,
                          paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")
  cat("WILCOXON SIGNED-RANK TEST:\n")
  cat("  V-statistic:", w_result$statistic, "\n")
  cat("  p-value:", format.pval(w_result$p.value, digits = 4), "\n\n")
} else {
  cat("Insufficient complete pairs for statistical testing.\n\n")
}

# ============================================================================
# APPROACH 2: SUBSTITUTE NON-DETECTS WITH DETECTION LIMIT
# ============================================================================
cat("========================================\n")
cat("APPROACH 2: Substituting Non-Detects with Detection Limit\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Substitute NA with detection limit
before_subst <- ifelse(is.na(before_conc), detection_limit_conc, before_conc)
after_subst <- ifelse(is.na(after_conc), detection_limit_conc, after_conc)

cat("This provides a CONSERVATIVE (minimum) estimate of depletion.\n")
cat("Non-detects are assumed to be at the detection limit.\n\n")

t_result_subst <- t.test(before_subst, after_subst,
                         paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")

cat("PAIRED T-TEST (with substitution):\n")
cat("  n pairs:", length(before_subst), "\n")
cat("  Mean Before:", round(mean(before_subst), 3), "ng/μL\n")
cat("  Mean After:", round(mean(after_subst), 3), "ng/μL\n")
cat("  t-statistic:", round(t_result_subst$statistic, 3), "\n")
cat("  df:", t_result_subst$parameter, "\n")
cat("  p-value:", format.pval(t_result_subst$p.value, digits = 4), "\n")

if (t_result_subst$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("  *** SIGNIFICANT even with conservative substitution ***\n")
} else {
  cat("  Not significant with conservative substitution\n")
}

differences_subst <- before_subst - after_subst
cohens_d_subst <- mean(differences_subst) / sd(differences_subst)
fold_change_subst <- mean(before_subst) / mean(after_subst)

cat("\nEFFECT SIZE (with substitution):\n")
cat("  Fold reduction:", round(fold_change_subst, 2), "×\n")
cat("  Cohen's d:", round(cohens_d_subst, 2), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# APPROACH 3: SIGN TEST (Most appropriate for non-detects!)
# ============================================================================
cat("========================================\n")
cat("APPROACH 3: Sign Test (Recommended for Non-Detects)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("The Sign Test only considers DIRECTION of change, not magnitude.\n")
cat("This is ideal when you have non-detects (complete depletion)!\n\n")

# Determine direction of change for each pair
# If after is ND and before is detected, that's a reduction
# If after is detected and before is ND, that's an increase (shouldn't happen)
# If both are ND, exclude from sign test

changes <- rep(NA, length(before_conc))
for (i in 1:length(before_conc)) {
  if (!is.na(before_conc[i]) && !is.na(after_conc[i])) {
    # Both detected - compare values
    changes[i] <- ifelse(before_conc[i] > after_conc[i], "reduction", "increase")
  } else if (!is.na(before_conc[i]) && is.na(after_conc[i])) {
    # Before detected, after ND - clear reduction
    changes[i] <- "reduction"
  } else if (is.na(before_conc[i]) && !is.na(after_conc[i])) {
    # Before ND, after detected - increase (unexpected)
    changes[i] <- "increase"
  }
  # If both ND, leave as NA
}

cat("Direction of change for each replicate:\n")
for (i in 1:length(changes)) {
  if (!is.na(changes[i])) {
    cat(sprintf("  Replicate %d: %s\n", cheese_paired$Replicate_ID[i], changes[i]))
  }
}
cat("\n")

# Count reductions vs increases
n_reductions <- sum(changes == "reduction", na.rm = TRUE)
n_increases <- sum(changes == "increase", na.rm = TRUE)
n_total <- n_reductions + n_increases

cat("SIGN TEST RESULTS:\n")
cat("  Total pairs with change:", n_total, "\n")
cat("  Reductions:", n_reductions, "\n")
cat("  Increases:", n_increases, "\n")

# Binomial test (H0: probability of reduction = 0.5)
if (n_total > 0) {
  sign_test <- binom.test(n_reductions, n_total, p = 0.5, alternative = "greater")
  cat("  p-value:", format.pval(sign_test$p.value, digits = 4), "\n")
  
  if (sign_test$p.value < 0.05) {
    cat("  *** SIGNIFICANT: Consistent direction of reduction (p < 0.05) ***\n")
  } else if (sign_test$p.value < 0.10) {
    cat("  † Trend toward consistent reduction (p < 0.10)\n")
  } else {
    cat("  Not significant\n")
  }
  
  cat("\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
  if (n_reductions == n_total) {
    cat("  ALL samples showed reduction in chicken DNA.\n")
    cat("  This includes samples with complete depletion (non-detect).\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  %d out of %d samples showed reduction.\n", n_reductions, n_total))
  }
} else {
  cat("  No pairs available for sign test.\n")
}

cat("\n")

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY AND RECOMMENDATION
# ============================================================================
cat("========================================\n")
cat("SUMMARY AND RECOMMENDATION\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("For publication, we recommend reporting:\n\n")
cat("1. PRIMARY ANALYSIS: Approach 1 (excluding non-detects)\n")
cat("   - Most conservative and standard approach\n")
cat("   - Report mean values and statistical test results\n\n")

cat("2. QUALITATIVE OBSERVATION:\n")
cat("   - Note in text: 'X samples showed no detectable chicken DNA\n")
cat("     after depletion (Ct > 40), indicating complete or near-complete\n")
cat("     depletion.'\n\n")

cat("3. SUPPLEMENTARY: Sign Test (Approach 3)\n")
cat("   - Shows consistent DIRECTION of effect across all samples\n")
cat("   - Particularly powerful when you have complete depletions\n\n")

cat("AVOID: Simply substituting detection limit values (Approach 2)\n")
cat("       unless you have a strong scientific justification.\n\n")
```

# Filtered Samples

```{r filtered combined panel plot}
# Check what samples we have in filtered category
cat("Checking filtered samples in data:\n")
data_clean %>% 
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("Centrifuge + Filter", "Filter Only")) %>%
  count(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID) %>%
  print()

# Prepare filtered data for plotting
filtered_data <- data_clean %>%
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("Centrifuge + Filter", "Filter Only")) %>%
  mutate(
    Below_Detection = is.na(Ct),
    Ct_plot = ifelse(is.na(Ct), detection_limit_ct, Ct),
    Conc_plot = ifelse(is.na(Concentration), detection_limit_conc, Concentration)
  )

# Expanded color palette for more replicates
color_palette_extended <- c("1" = "#E64B35", "2" = "#4DBBD5", 
                            "3" = "#00A087", "4" = "#3C5488",
                            "5" = "#F39B7F", "6" = "#8491B4")

# Create long format data for faceting
filtered_long <- filtered_data %>%
  select(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID, Below_Detection, Ct_plot, Conc_plot) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Ct_plot, Conc_plot),
    names_to = "Measurement",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Measurement = factor(Measurement,
                        levels = c("Ct_plot", "Conc_plot"),
                        labels = c("Ct Value", 
                                  "DNA Concentration (ng/µL) - log10 scale"))
  )

# Create the combined panel plot
filtered_panel_plot <- ggplot(filtered_long,
                              aes(x = Treatment, y = Value,
                                  color = as.factor(Replicate_ID),
                                  group = as.factor(Replicate_ID))) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", 
               width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2) +
  facet_grid(Measurement ~ Sample_Type, scales = "free_y", switch = "y") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_palette_extended,
                     name = "Replicate") +
  labs(
    title = "Filtered Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy",
    subtitle = "Each point = qPCR technical replicate | Lines connect mean ± SE | Concentration on log scale",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

print(filtered_panel_plot)
ggsave("filtered_combined_panels.png", filtered_panel_plot,
       width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

# Enriched Samples

```{r enriched combined panel plot}
# Check what samples we have in enriched category
cat("Checking enriched samples in data:\n")
data_clean %>% 
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("RVS", "CAT")) %>%
  count(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID) %>%
  print()

# Prepare enriched data for plotting
enriched_data <- data_clean %>%
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("RVS", "CAT")) %>%
  mutate(
    Below_Detection = is.na(Ct),
    Ct_plot = ifelse(is.na(Ct), detection_limit_ct, Ct),
    Conc_plot = ifelse(is.na(Concentration), detection_limit_conc, Concentration)
  )

# Create long format data for faceting
enriched_long <- enriched_data %>%
  select(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID, Below_Detection, Ct_plot, Conc_plot) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Ct_plot, Conc_plot),
    names_to = "Measurement",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Measurement = factor(Measurement,
                        levels = c("Ct_plot", "Conc_plot"),
                        labels = c("Ct Value", 
                                  "DNA Concentration (ng/µL) - log10 scale"))
  )

# Create the combined panel plot
enriched_panel_plot <- ggplot(enriched_long,
                              aes(x = Treatment, y = Value,
                                  color = as.factor(Replicate_ID),
                                  group = as.factor(Replicate_ID))) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", 
               width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2) +
  facet_grid(Measurement ~ Sample_Type, scales = "free_y", switch = "y") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_palette_extended,
                     name = "Replicate") +
  labs(
    title = "Enriched Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy",
    subtitle = "Each point = qPCR technical replicate | Lines connect mean ± SE | Concentration on log scale",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

print(enriched_panel_plot)
ggsave("enriched_combined_panels.png", enriched_panel_plot,
       width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

# Summary Report

```{r summary table}
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("FINAL SUMMARY\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Create comprehensive summary table
final_summary <- summary_stats %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n_bio_reps = n(),
    n_detected = sum(!All_Non_Detect),
    n_complete_ND = sum(All_Non_Detect, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_Ct = mean(Mean_Ct, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_Conc = mean(Mean_Conc, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 3)))

print(final_summary)

cat("\nAll plots saved as PNG files in working directory.\n\n")
cat("Key improvements in this analysis:\n")
cat("1. Proper handling of non-detections in statistical tests\n")
cat("2. Three statistical approaches presented (exclude, substitute, sign test)\n")
cat("3. Combined panel plots showing Ct + Concentration together\n")
cat("4. Lines connecting means for each biological replicate\n")
cat("5. Clear marking of detection limits\n")
cat("6. Better color scheme matching your previous plots\n\n")

sessionInfo()
```
